Important Metrics\n\n* `Score` - An estimation of how good indexing pressure  is balanced\n    - Higher is better, from 1-5\n* `All Max Dev` and `Coord Max Dev` How well balanced is indexing pressure\n    - Lower is better, good is below 1.5, ideal is below 1\n\n#### Explanation\n\nThe `indexing_pressure` metrics measure the current amount of heap memory used for indexing operations. This is broken down into four categories:\n\n1. `coordinating` - Memory used for routing indexing requests\n2. `primary` - Indexing for primary shards\n3. `replica` - Indexing for replica shards\n4. `all` - Total of the above\n\nIn an ideal balance, the `primary + replica` indexing pressure on all nodes will be similar.\n\nSeparately, the `coordinating` pressure should also be similar across all nodes. Where `primary` and `replica` require shards to be allocated to that node, the `coordinating` pressure does not; _any_ node can coordinate a request.\n\nIf there are nodes with _no_ `coordinating` pressure, this could be an indicator of poor load balancing or network
